name: ISO11783 Interaction Layer
title: ISO11783 Interaction Layer
tables: ISO11783 IL To use the CAPL functions the node layer Iso11783_IL.dll must be included. You can include the node layer by using the configuration dialog of the node on page Components or via the node attribute NodeLayerModules in the database. More information about setting up the ISO11783 Interaction Layer you can found here.

Note The following functions are supported by both the TIM Client and the TIM Server. Many functions refer to a specific client/server connection. The counterpart is defined by a node from the data basis. The ISO11783 device name of the node specified in the database must match the device name of the ECU.
Removes all keys of the LwA (lightweight authentication) table.
Connects the automation state of a TIM function to a system variable.
Connects the state of a TIM client/server connection to a system variable.
Connects the synchronization point of a TIM client/server connection to a system variable.
Continues authentication process which has been frozen by Iso11783IL_TIMFreezeConnection.
Freezes authentication process at a specific state.

Stores the table with lightweight authentication (LwA) keys in a file.

This function returns the address of the Working Set Master, which is assigned to an ECU with the address ecuAddress.
Use this function to set the address of the Working Set Master, if this ECU is a member of a working set.
